title: Nitrogen management in soybean (Glycine max (L.)) and pigeonpea (Cajanus cajan( L.))
abstract:
Soybean (Glycine mm (L) Merrill) crop is becoming increasingly popular in
central India, particularly in the Vertisol (Black soil) region. Today it commands an area of
5.6 m ha in the states of Madhya Pradesh and Maharastra Some area is also planted to the
crop in Uttar Pradesh and Andhra Pradesh. An analysis of the area and yield trend of soybean
in India show that although the area expanded at a compound rate of 600 per cent, the
productivity increase is negligible. The crop yield was low, 862 kg ha.' Pigeonpea (Cajamrs cajm
(L) Millsp) is an important grain legume crop of the semi arid tropics (SAT) India is the major pigeonpea producing country accounting for over 90 per cent of the global pigeonpea
production. The productivity of the crop in India is low, 533 kg ha.'...
